own_review.issue: polyglot-common bumped to 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT, which depends on the unmerged upstream polyglot-maven PR #242 (PR description admits the update is 'not yet functional'). A SNAPSHOT dependency on an unreleased third-party artifact is a merge/release blocker.

ThreadLocal leak: IS_FIRST.set(false) executes, but IS_FIRST.remove() is only reached inside the 'if (subFolders != null)' block after the loop. If dir.listFiles() returns null (I/O error/permissions) or locatePomReference throws inside the loop, the flag stays false for the thread's lifetime — every later top-level aggregator call on that thread silently takes the skip branch. Severe in thread-reusing environments (mvnd, IDE embedders, ITs in one JVM). Needs try/finally. Note the re-entrancy mechanism itself DOES work: nested locatePomReference calls re-enter this mapping on the same thread with the flag false, which is exactly the intended 'skip nested' behavior.

The tycho.pomless.aggregator.names system property is removed without deprecation; existing user configurations setting it are now silently ignored, and no documentation/RELEASE_NOTES entry records the breaking change.

Behavior regression vs COMMON_NAMES: aggregators nested inside aggregators (e.g. releng/sites, bundles/tests — previously matched anywhere by name) are no longer discovered, since nested folders without poms are skipped during a scan. Intentional per the comment, but a silent regression for existing multi-level layouts that deserves explicit documentation.

assertMentionedDirectories asserts an exact ORDER of skipped folders ('target', 'otherFolder', 'target'), but the order comes from File.listFiles(), whose ordering is unspecified (e.g. hash order on ext4). Potentially flaky across filesystems; should compare order-insensitively or sort.

isValidLocation switched from String.endsWith to Path.of(location).endsWith — now matches only an exact last path segment ('feature.xml'/'pom.tycho'), no longer arbitrary suffixes like 'myfeature.xml'. A correctness tightening, but a behavior change worth confirming; Path.of may also throw InvalidPathException on malformed location strings. TychoTargetMapping still uses String.endsWith (inconsistent).

testPomlessTestPluginDetection downgraded from 'clean test' to 'clean compile' — the IT no longer verifies that the detected eclipse-test-plugin packaging actually drives test execution; unrelated to this PR's purpose. (The removed verifyTextInLog("") was a no-op.)

points.point: ThreadLocal state leak: IS_FIRST.remove() nested inside if(subFolders!=null); listFiles()==null leaves flag false permanently, subsequent aggregators silently skipped; fix with try/finally
points.note: Verified real — exact leak path and correct fix; the centerpiece bug of this PR.

points.point: ThreadLocal persists across Maven builds in thread-reusing daemon/IDE mode
points.note: As stated ('never re-initialized') is inaccurate — remove() resets it on the happy path; only the leak from point 1 makes it persist. Corollary/amplifier of point 1, not an independent issue.

points.point: IS_FIRST does not enforce 'directly nested' semantics; a bundle with a stale pom.tycho processed first would cause the aggregator's first real directory to be skipped entirely
points.note: Fabricated failure scenario: each top-level locatePom call starts with the flag true (remove() resets after every successful scan), and per-thread re-entrancy IS exactly the directly-nested semantics. The claimed skip cannot occur outside the already-reported leak.

points.point: Path.of(location).endsWith(TYCHO_POM) tightens matching from suffix to exact last segment in TychoAggregatorMapping
points.note: Verified real semantic change, accurately described.

points.point: Same Path-based matching change in TychoFeatureMapping
points.note: Duplicate of the previous observation for a second file.

points.point: Removed default getPriority() in base class; verified all five subclasses override it
points.note: Accurate positive verification, not an issue.

points.point: Unrelated test scope change: testPomlessTestPluginDetection switched from 'test' to 'compile' goals
points.note: Real observation but framed only as commit hygiene, not as the (mild) coverage loss.

points.point: polyglot-common 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT depends on unreleased polyglot-maven PR #242; blocks CI/merge
points.note: Verified blocker — cannot be merged/released against an unpublished SNAPSHOT.

missed: Human's point: polyglot XmlMapping.accept() falsely accepts any *.xml (including feature.xml) by extension; explicit feature priority is needed because the previous safe ordering was a plexus implementation-detail accident
Silent removal of tycho.pomless.aggregator.names is a breaking change for users with no deprecation/RELEASE_NOTES entry
Nested-aggregator regression: aggregators inside aggregators previously matched via COMMON_NAMES are no longer discovered
New log-order assertion depends on unspecified File.listFiles() ordering — potentially flaky test
exceeds: ThreadLocal leak with exact mechanism and try/finally fix (not in human reference)
SNAPSHOT dependency merge-blocker (not in human reference)
Path.of last-segment matching tightening (not in human reference)

points.point: IS_FIRST ThreadLocal logic is broken — withInitial re-invokes the supplier after remove() so get() always returns true; the 'Skip nested folder' else branch is unreachable and the new test assertions will fail
points.note: Fabricated. The skip branch runs on RE-ENTRANT calls: during a scan, locatePomReference re-enters this mapping on the same thread while the flag is still false (remove() only happens after the loop). The mechanism works and the test passes.

points.point: IS_FIRST is per-thread; parallel builds (-T) cause duplicate scanning, defeating the optimization
points.note: False — IS_FIRST is a re-entrancy guard, not a global once-per-build flag; no directory is ever scanned twice because polyglot calls locatePom once per module dir. Per-thread scope is exactly correct. Built on the same misreading as point 1.

points.point: Redundant getPriority() override (+1) in TychoFeatureMapping — AbstractXMLTychoMapping already returns 1; dead code, remove it
points.note: Numerically verified (base class line 98-99 returns 1), but the override carries the comment documenting WHY feature must outrank polyglot's XmlMapping — removing it would erase exactly the invariant the human reviewer highlights. Accurate fact, counterproductive advice.

points.point: Removing getPriority() from AbstractTychoMapping breaks implicit contract for future subclasses (compile error instead of inherited 0)
points.note: Speculative forward-looking note; acknowledges all current subclasses override it.

points.point: Nit: Path.of(location).endsWith(...) marginally more robust but original String.endsWith was sufficient; avoid Path allocation
points.note: Misses the actual semantic point (suffix vs exact-last-segment matching) and even mixes up the constants (TYCHO_POM vs FEATURE_XML); recommendation points the wrong way but is not a fabricated defect.

points.point: Clean removal of -Dtycho.pomless.aggregator.names from maven.config; no other references remain in the codebase
points.note: Verified true (the constant is removed by this PR too), but it is a positive observation that overlooks the silent-breaking-change angle.

missed: ThreadLocal leak: IS_FIRST.remove() skipped when listFiles() returns null or the loop throws — the real bug (v7 instead fabricated a different, nonexistent bug in the same code)
polyglot-common 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT dependency on unmerged upstream PR — merge/release blocker, never mentioned
Human's point: XmlMapping.accept() accepts any *.xml by extension; the explicit feature priority guards a previously accidental ordering (v7 touched the override but recommended deleting it)
Nested-aggregator regression vs old COMMON_NAMES behavior
Log-order test assertion depends on unspecified File.listFiles() ordering
exceeds: Verified AbstractXMLTychoMapping base priority value in the repo (accurate fact-check beyond the diff)
Verified no remaining references to the removed aggregator.names property

qualitative: v5 delivers both genuinely real, high-value findings — the ThreadLocal remove() leak with the exact failure path and fix, and the 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT merge blocker — plus an accurate note on the Path.of matching change, but it burns credit on a fabricated 'first real directory gets skipped' scenario and misses the human's XmlMapping-accept insight along with the property-removal breaking change, nested-aggregator regression, and the order-dependent log assertion. v7 is substantially worse: its headline claim that the IS_FIRST else-branch is unreachable and the PR's own test will fail is a confident fabrication that inverts reality (the skip branch fires on re-entrant calls before remove()), its -T duplicate-scanning point repeats the same misreading, and it completely misses both real blockers (the ThreadLocal leak and the SNAPSHOT dependency). v7's one distinctive contribution — verifying that AbstractXMLTychoMapping already returns priority 1 — is factually right but leads to advice (delete the override) that would erase the documented invariant the human reviewer explains. Net: v5 0, v7 -7; v5 clearly stronger on this PR.
